behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at module handoff (inventory availability filter + ranking output merge), not a single-function rule. No behavior text requires real HTTP/OS/subprocess or AI model output. Rejected System because the text only says exclusion in results, not an external contract or infrastructure condition.
behaviors.test_description: Run the recommendation pipeline end-to-end in-process with catalog, inventory, and ranker modules wired together; include high-score out-of-stock items and assert they never appear in returned recommendations.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about propagation from user settings into filtering, a cross-module handoff risk. No real infra dependency is stated, and no AI/ML judgment is involved. Rejected System because behavior text is limited to filtering correctness, not external API/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire user profile/settings loader with filtering and recommendation assembly; set blocked categories in settings and assert returned recommendations contain none of those categories.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This is ranking behavior that depends on interaction of feature extraction (recent browsing) and scorer/ranker modules. It is not an LLM/ML-service call requirement in the behavior text; it reads as deterministic engine logic. Rejected Agentic because no real model call or non-deterministic model judgment is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Construct candidates with controlled browsing-signal features through the real feature pipeline + ranker wiring, then assert signal-matching products rank above otherwise comparable no-signal products.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Stability depends on ordering + pagination modules working together over unchanged data, which unit tests can miss at composition boundaries. No explicit requirement for real network/process/OS. Rejected System because behavior text defines deterministic in-service behavior, not external protocol/runtime conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Generate a fixed recommendation set, request page 2 twice via the assembled recommendation service layer, and assert identical item IDs/order and correct slice boundaries both times.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: This is a business-rule constraint enforced by candidate diversification + ranking composition. It is not inherently a real-model quality test unless behavior explicitly depends on external ML outputs. Rejected Agentic because the text specifies a deterministic requirement ('at least one product from unseen category'), not model judgment quality from a real AI API.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full in-process recommendation assembly (candidate generation, diversification/serendipity rule, ranking) for a user with narrow browsing history; assert at least one returned item is from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback is an orchestration path: history detection + popularity source + result assembly. This is cross-module and can fail despite unit tests. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external API behavior, only fallback correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke recommendation generation for a user with empty history using wired history detector and popularity provider modules; assert non-empty results and that ordering follows popularity inputs.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is data handoff from recommendation response context to tracking persistence schema, which requires module wiring and storage write/read path. Rejected System because the behavior text does not specify real HTTP/external service contracts; a local DB-backed integration round-trip can validate the fields.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate recommendation delivery and click events through tracking service + repository with a real local test DB; read back stored events and assert product_id, rank_position, and timestamp correctness for each click.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Latency target depends on real runtime/infra effects (CPU, memory, IO characteristics) that mocks and pure in-process unit checks can hide. This is a real performance behavior, not only logic correctness. Rejected Integration because synthetic in-process stubs can miss true end-to-end timing behavior under realistic load/data size.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end performance test in a production-like runtime with a 100k-product dataset, measure request latency over repeated recommendation calls, and verify p95 (or defined SLO metric) is under 200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving an externally visible cross-request side effect (purchase event impacts later recommendation output) via round-trip observation, matching the System round-trip rule. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires subsequent requests reflect persisted state change, which is exactly the external effect to verify end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real purchase action through the service boundary, then issue a subsequent recommendation request for the same user and assert the purchased product's rank is lower (or removed per policy) compared with pre-purchase output.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Determinism here is about composed pipeline stability (feature snapshot + ranking + ordering) across repeated calls with unchanged state. No explicit real external infra or AI model dependence is stated. Rejected System because the behavior text is state-stability logic, not external protocol/runtime fault behavior.
behaviors.test_description: With a fixed persisted user state and catalog snapshot, call the recommendation service twice in-process (same inputs, no mutations) and assert identical ordered product IDs across both responses.
